items.title: Telemetry dashboard — heartbeat collection + task_board fleet view
items.context_summary: Add heartbeat.v1.jsonl emission to all smart tools, enhance task_board to read heartbeats and show fleet status with per-agent phase timelines and stale detection.
items.next_step: Done. Verify in next session.

sessions.note: TEL-001 implemented: Bus events now published at all 4 task lifecycle points (running, completed, failed x2). Schema file created. task.ts now emits TaskStatusChanged events via Bus system. Pre-existing TaskStatusChanged event definition in coord-events.ts now has its first subscriber (publisher).

items.title: Contention detection — shared file auto-detection in produce_fragment
items.context_summary: When two lanes touch the same file via produce_fragment, the system should auto-detect and flag the contention. Anchor adjustment should happen silently; only real collisions escalate.
items.next_step: Design the contention data model — what does a collision look like in the fragment protocol?

sessions.note: Contention detection implemented. Created contention.ts (267 lines) with in-memory fragment tracking, anchor overlap heuristics (numeric + named), collision Bus events. Added 'contention' operation to CoordinationTool. coord-events.ts consolidated with all 6 event definitions.

items.title: Secretary (lane-owner) agent — delegate a full lane lifecycle
items.context_summary: Lane-owner agent receives a mission from orchestrator, runs full wave lifecycle, reports back via structured handoff/blocker/alert messages. Supports cancel, pivot, and warm resume.
items.next_step: Done. Test in next multi-lane session.

items.title: Cross-session roadmap tools — active/blueprint/audit system
items.context_summary: Five tools: roadmap_init (startup injection), roadmap_progress (update status), roadmap_next (dependency-resolved next actions), roadmap_deprecate (mark obsolete), roadmap_prioritize (reorder). File structure: blueprint (rarely updated), active.v1.json (frequently updated), progress.v1.jsonl (append-only audit).
items.next_step: Create the 5 roadmap tools and the initial blueprint.

items.title: Lessons register — cross-session pattern memory
items.context_summary: lesson_register tool records one-sentence insights to lessons.v1.jsonl. Categories: codebase, workflow, architecture, tool, timing, convention. Future sessions read these at startup.
items.next_step: Done. Verify lessons are being recorded and discovered.

items.title: Session diff tool — consolidated change reporting
items.context_summary: session_diff tool aggregates edit_logs, runs git diff --stat, and produces per-package breakdown with file lists. Used for PR descriptions and handoffs.
items.next_step: Done. Verify output quality in next session.

items.title: Complete Drizzle schema definitions for all tables on Postgres
items.context_summary: account.pg.sql.ts exists untracked, account.sql.ts has staged TimestampsPg changes. ~6 tables need Pg schema definitions. Must be dual-write compatible with SQLite during migration.
items.next_step: Inventory remaining tables, finish Pg schema for each, add dual-write validation.

sessions.note: Completed Phase 1 — ToolCache: New ToolCache service (~80 lines), wired into tool.ts/registry.ts/app-runtime.ts, 8 cacheable tools marked, typecheck passes with 0 new errors.

Schema definitions verified 100% complete — all 7 SQLite tables have complete Postgres equivalents. The remaining 60% is the application layer migration (production code switching imports from .sql.ts to .pg.sql.ts + dual-write plumbing). Schema layer is ready — deployment layer needs the pipeline work.
Schemas complete. Next step: application layer — switch production imports from .sql.ts to .pg.sql.ts + implement dual-write in the DB adapter layer.

Session built complete Runtime Event infrastructure: Event Persistence (schema, store, bridge), Timeline Inspector UI (7 components + route), DuckDB Analytics (6 views), Debug Export + Flight Recorder. Pre-fabricated next-wave UX and Agent Workbench lanes.

items.title: Migration runner with dual SQLite+Pg CI support
items.context_summary: Need migration runner that can target either SQLite or Pg based on env. Test suite runs against both. CI matrix: SQLite (fast feedback) + Pg (correctness).
items.next_step: Design migration runner API — how does it know which dialect to use?

items.title: Validate all Drizzle queries execute on Postgres — CI with Pg service
items.context_summary: 27+ test files use SQLite. Need Pg test targets. Prior session added account.pg.sql.ts but couldn't run tests because CI lacks Postgres service container.
items.next_step: Add Postgres service to CI workflow, tag tests with @pg, run subset against Pg.

items.title: Production Postgres connection pool and health checks
items.context_summary: Connection pool with retry logic, health check endpoint, graceful degradation if Pg is unreachable.
items.next_step: Design connection pool API — Effect Layer or class-based?

sessions.note: Production Postgres connection pool implemented: PgPool service with retry+error classification, health check, graceful degradation to SQLite, pool config via env vars, and test suite.
PgPool service with retry (3 attempts, exponential backoff), error classification (auth/SSL skip retry), health check (SELECT 1), graceful degradation to SQLite. All 20/20 tests pass.

items.title: Extract Electron assumptions from config resolution
items.context_summary: Desktop app assumes Electron everywhere. Config resolution assumes filesystem paths (app.getPath). Need to extract the Electron-specific parts behind an interface so the renderer can run in Chrome.
items.next_step: Audit all app.getPath calls, classify them as Electron-only vs portable.

items.title: Renderer loads in Chrome without Electron — definition of done
items.context_summary: Goal: open localhost:5173 in Chrome and see the full app UI working. No Electron APIs in the renderer code path. This is the decoupling verification milestone.
items.next_step: List every Electron API used in the renderer. For each: mock, polyfill, or extract.

items.title: Plugin system decoupled from Electron IPC
items.context_summary: Plugins currently communicate via Electron IPC channels. Need abstract transport layer so plugins work in Chrome (postMessage) and Electron (ipcRenderer).
items.next_step: Design PluginTransport interface — what does postMessage look like vs ipcRenderer?

items.title: Break up 503-line ipc.ts god module
items.context_summary: Phase 2 extracted github-ipc.ts from ipc.ts. The remaining 503 lines are a god module mixing MCP handlers, session management, file system ops, and window controls. Each concern needs its own module.
items.next_step: Inventory ipc.ts by concern: how many distinct handler groups exist?

items.title: Dynamic menu actions — not hardcoded desktop menu
items.context_summary: Desktop menu actions are hardcoded in the main process. Should be registered by feature modules, similar to how IPC handlers are registered.
items.next_step: Design menu action registry — what does a feature module declare?

items.title: Preload bridge type generation — not manually typed
items.context_summary: The preload bridge (exposing main-process APIs to renderer) is manually typed. If an IPC handler changes signature, the bridge type silently drifts. Generate types from handler definitions.
items.next_step: Map all IPC handler signatures → generate bridge type from source of truth.

items.title: Dead code removal sweep
items.context_summary: After extraction and decoupling, unused imports, dead modules, and zombie code paths will be revealed. Remove them.
items.next_step: Run ts-prune or similar after HC-001 and HC-002 complete.

items.title: Dialog component test harness
items.context_summary: Phase 2 added 54 IPC tests but zero dialog component tests. Need a SolidJS dialog test harness: mount, interact, assert visible/hidden, assert callbacks fired.
items.next_step: Build test harness: mountDialog(), clickButton(), assertDialogVisible().

items.title: 15+ dialog component tests
items.context_summary: 15+ SolidJS dialogs with zero test coverage: dialog-edit-agent, dialog-select-mcp, dialog-mcp-form, dialog-settings, dialog-export, dialog-import, dialog-delete-confirm, and more.
items.next_step: Write tests for each dialog: smoke (renders), interaction (buttons work), state (form values persist).

items.title: Locale fallback chain
items.context_summary: github.* keys exist in only 1 of 17 locales. Need a fallback chain: requested locale → parent locale (zh-CN → zh) → base (en). Missing keys silently fall through instead of showing key names.
items.next_step: Implement fallback chain in the i18n provider.

items.title: Parity tests across all 17 locales
items.context_summary: Test that every locale has keys for all user-facing strings. Missing keys → test failure. Tests run in CI and block PRs that add English keys without locale stubs.
items.next_step: Build key inventory script: extract all t() calls, check each locale file.

items.title: Bulk stub generation for missing locale keys
items.context_summary: Script that reads English locale, finds missing keys in other locales, generates stubs with [LANG] marker. Human translators replace markers, but the app is usable immediately.
items.next_step: Build stub generator: read en.ts → for each target locale → emit missing keys as stubs.
